name: Dharmaraja Adhvarindra
dates_notes: Late 16th – early 17th c.; dated from manuscripts and citations.
core_thesis: Dharmarāja Adhvarīndra is the great pedagogical systematiser of Vivaraṇa-Advaita epistemology. Working downstream of Citsukha and Madhusūdana, he organises the Advaitic theory of *pramāṇas* into a single teachable architecture in the technical idiom of pre-Gaṅgeśa and early Navya-Nyāya. Four positions anchor the system. First, Advaita accepts the six Bhāṭṭa *pramāṇas* — *pratyakṣa*, *anumāna*, *upamāna*, *śabda*, *arthāpatti*, *anupalabdhi* — at the *vyāvahārika* level. Second, *pratyakṣa* is analysed by *vṛtti-vyāpti*: the *antaḥkaraṇa-vṛtti* extends through the senses to the object, and the consciousness underlying the *vṛtti* (*vṛtti-caitanya*) becomes non-different from the consciousness underlying the object (*viṣaya-caitanya*). Third, only the Mahāvākya is *brahma-pramāṇa*; it generates the *akhaṇḍākāra-vṛtti* whose function is to remove avidyā, though knowledge itself is never produced. Fourth, the Naiyāyika analyses of *pratyakṣa*, *anumāna*, and erroneous cognition are refuted in detail. Dharmarāja stands firmly in the Vivaraṇa line on the locus-of-avidyā question and writes downstream of Citsukha and Madhusūdana.

engaged_works.title: Vedanta-Paribhasha
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored; the standard manual of Advaita epistemology.
engaged_works.summary: The standard manual — *paribhāṣā*, 'technical-vocabulary handbook' — of Advaita epistemology, organised in eight *paricchedas*: one per *pramāṇa* (*pratyakṣa*, *anumāna*, *upamāna*, *āgama* / *śabda*, *arthāpatti*, *anupalabdhi*), plus *prameya* and *prayojana*. Dharmarāja gives the canonical Advaitic *vṛtti-vyāpti* analysis of perception: the *antaḥkaraṇa* extends through the senses, conforms to the object's shape, and the consciousness underlying *vṛtti* becomes non-different from the consciousness underlying the object. The Advaita treatment of *anumāna* is developed in the language of pre-Gaṅgeśa and early Navya-Nyāya. The Mahāvākya is given the standard exposition as *brahma-pramāṇa* generating the *akhaṇḍākāra-vṛtti*. The doctrine of *prātibhāsika*, *vyāvahārika*, and *pāramārthika* levels of reality is formulated. The work is the universal entry-point to technical Advaita epistemology in modern *Vedānta* curricula and one of the most-commented texts of late Advaita. Rāmakṛṣṇa Adhvarī's *Śikhāmaṇi*, with the *Maṇiprabhā* sub-commentary, is the standard apparatus.
